data.title: U.S. Senate Rejects Stablecoin Regulation Framework Amid Controversy
data.description:
According to PANews, the U.S. Senate has narrowly rejected the GENIUS Act, a proposed framework for stablecoin regulation, with a vote of 48 to 49. The bill faced opposition from the Democratic Party, which cited concerns over potential corruption risks linked to U.S. President Donald Trump's family's involvement in cryptocurrency. Despite the Republican majority, the bill failed to reach the 60-vote procedural threshold required for passage.

The GENIUS Act aimed to establish the first regulatory system for stablecoins in the United States. However, the Democrats opposed the bill, arguing that it lacked provisions to prohibit government officials from holding cryptocurrency assets. In response to the setback, Senate Republicans have indicated plans to reintroduce the bill for a vote in the coming days.

data.title: SEC’s crypto task force lead criticizes ETF approval process, highlights need for patience
data.description:
The head of the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Crypto Task Force, Hester Peirce, said the decade-long process that led to the approval of spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) was mishandled, and urged crypto market participants to be patient with ongoing regulatory delays.
source: https://cryptoslate.com/secs-crypto-task-force-lead-criticizes-etf-approval-process-highlights-need-for-patience/

data.title: Trump Proposes Tax Increase for Wealthiest Americans
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. President Donald Trump is advocating for a tax increase on the wealthiest Americans to balance other tax cuts in his economic plan. The proposal suggests a new tax bracket of 39.6% for individuals earning at least $2.5 million annually or couples earning $5 million. If approved by Congress, this would restore the top tax rate to its pre-2017 levels, reversing Trump's previous tax cuts. The current top individual tax rate stands at 37%. Trump made this proposal during a phone call with House Speaker Johnson on Wednesday, reiterating his desire to eliminate the carried interest tax break for venture capital and private equity fund managers.

data.title: Wellgistics Health integrates XRP into payment network to improve medical payment efficiency
data.description: Wellgistics Health announced that it will integrate XRP and related technologies into its healthcare provider payment network to "streamline transactions between pharmacies, medical suppliers, and prescription drug manufacturers." The company emphasized that XRP's transaction "finality time" and "transaction costs are only a fraction of a cent" are far superior to traditional financial systems such as ACH or wire transfers. The integration will "reduce cross-border friction" and "allow transactions between different companies in the supply chain to be settled in real time."

data.title: Meta is considering integrating stablecoins to reduce payment costs after selling the Diem project
data.description: Meta is considering integrating stablecoins to reduce the cost of payments compared to fiat currencies, such as paying Instagram creators. The report said that "the company does not seem to have yet to determine which stablecoin to use". In addition, Meta has "contacted crypto infrastructure companies to solve the cost of cross-regional payments". The company has also hired "former Ripple executive Ginger Baker as vice president of product". The renewed focus on stablecoins comes at a time when "US regulators have taken a friendlier attitude towards cryptocurrencies after President Trump took office". In 2019, Meta launched the stablecoin project Libra, which was later renamed Diem, but "due to regulatory suppression, the project failed to launch as scheduled and was eventually closed". Diem's ​​intellectual property has been sold to Silvergate Bank, and although the bank later declared bankruptcy, "the project continues in new chains such as Aptos in the MOVE language, but no longer involves Meta."

data.title: Trump: Talking to Fed Chairman Powell is like "playing the lute to a cow"
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: US President Trump said that everyone except the Federal Reserve is cutting interest rates. Talking to Federal Reserve Chairman Powell is like "playing the lute to a cow". Federal Reserve Chairman Powell is always "too late". Federal Reserve Chairman Powell may not like me. Even without cutting interest rates, the United States is still doing well.
